name: Upside-Down Brunch Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix together the flour, cornmeal, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the egg, milk, and oil until combined. Set aside.
3. Heat the cooking oil in a 1-quart microwave-safe casserole on high for 30 to 45 seconds or until melted. Stir in the brown sugar until fully incorporated.
4. Add the pineapple and cook uncovered on high for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring once, until bubbly.
5. Stir in the ham. Pour the milk mixture over the dry ingredients and stir just until the batter is smooth (avoid overmixing).
6. Spoon the batter over the hot sugar mixture. Cook uncovered at medium power (50%) for 6 minutes, rotating the dish halfway through.
7. Increase the heat to high and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
8. Using a wooden toothpick, test the center of the cake near the wet surface. It should feel firm when touched with the toothpick.
9. If needed, continue cooking on high power for up to 2 more minutes until done.
10. Allow the cake to stand uncovered for 10 minutes before inverting it onto a serving platter.
11. Cut into wedges and serve warm with syrup. Serves 6.
(Preparation time: 15 minutes)
